fact: Article 124 establishes the Supreme Court of India, which was inaugurated on January 28, 1950, replacing the Federal Court of India (1937).
relevance: Frequently asked static GK question in SSC Tier-1.

fact: Article 32 gives the Supreme Court writ jurisdiction for Fundamental Rights, while Article 226 gives High Courts wider writ jurisdiction for Fundamental Rights AND legal rights.
relevance: High-yield core comparison topic in SSC CGL.

fact: Justice P.N. Bhagwati and Justice V.R. Krishna Iyer are recognized as pioneers of Public Interest Litigation (PIL) in India.
relevance: Direct repeat question in SSC CGL and CPO exams.

fact: The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) under Article 148 is called the 'Guardian of the Public Purse' and submits 3 audit reports to the President.
relevance: Repeatedly tested under CAG powers and reporting duties.

fact: NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission on January 1, 2015, and is a Non-Constitutional, Non-Statutory (Executive) body.
relevance: Crucial distinction asked under Constitutional vs Executive body questions.
